title: crotalaria quinquefolia
intro: Crotalaria quinquefolia, also known as the five-leaf crotalaria or five-leaf rattlebox, is a flowering plant species in the legume family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Crotalaria quinquefolia include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, due to its immunomodulatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or by using them in a decoction, and are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
